message:
fix(mux): prevent WebSocket receive loop blocking during file transfers

**Problem:**
Consecutive file downloads would hang because pushDownloadFiles() blocked
the WebSocket receive thread for 5+ seconds, preventing new TRANSFER_INIT
messages from being processed.

**Root Cause:**
handleTransferInit() called pushDownloadFiles() synchronously on the
WebSocket receive thread. While waiting for goroutines to finish file
compression/sending, the receive loop couldn't read new messages from
the TCP buffer, causing subsequent downloads to timeout.

**Solution:**
Spawn pushDownloadFiles() in a separate OS thread (line 3358 of main.zig)
so the WebSocket receive loop can continue processing incoming messages
while file transfers run in the background.

**Architecture:**
- OS thread: Runs pushDownloadFiles() to unblock WebSocket receive loop
- Greenthreads/Goroutines: Used by pushDownloadFiles() for parallel I/O
  (compressing 49+ files concurrently)

This hybrid approach leverages both thread types for their strengths:
OS threads for system-level blocking operations, goroutines for concurrent
application-level I/O.

**Changes:**
- main.zig: Thread pushDownloadFiles() to avoid blocking receive loop
- file-transfer.ts: OPFS persistence for download resume across page reloads
- transfer.zig: Server-side TRANSFER_RESUME protocol support
- mux.ts: Remove onConnectionShouldClose callback (rely on idle timer)
